attributes.content: Refer to Transfer Admission Screening Policy

notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.
notes.contentHtml:
THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: Transfer students who want to pursue the psychology major (general or intensive), must have applied and been admitted to UC Santa Cruz as a proposed psychology major. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the “ Required Major Preparation Courses ” section below, earning at least the minimum grade specified for each category by the end of the spring term prior to fall enrollment: Introduction to Psychology: minimum grade of B- (2.7)* Statistics: minimum grade of B- (2.7) Mathematics: minimum grade of C (2.0) *If a grade of C (2.0) or C+ (2.3) is earned in a course articulated to PSYC 1, the student must earn a minimum grade of B– (2.7) in a course articulated to PSYC 10 or PSYC 20. Refer to the “ Recommended Preparation ” section below for courses approved to fulfill these requirements. AP Exam Score Substitutions Qualifying AP exam scores may be used to satisfy certain required transfer screening courses. UCSC awards course credit based on the year the exam was taken. Exam credit policies are available on the Degree Progress Unit website . Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required major preparation courses, prospective transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses in the " Recommended Preparation " section below. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses prior to transfer may need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Students who cannot enroll in the articulated equivalent of PSYC 20 at a California Community College are strongly encouraged to take PSYC 20 at UC Santa Cruz the summer before their first fall quarter. For more information, please visit the UC Santa Cruz Summer Edge webpage . Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Psychology website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Psychology B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer students who want to pursue the psychology major (general or intensive), must have applied and been admitted to UC Santa Cruz as a proposed psychology major. 

﻿

Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the “Required Major Preparation Courses” section below, earning at least the minimum grade specified for each category by the end of the spring term prior to fall enrollment:


  • Introduction to Psychology: minimum grade of B- (2.7)*
  • Statistics: minimum grade of B- (2.7)
  • Mathematics: minimum grade of C (2.0)


*If a grade of C (2.0) or C+ (2.3) is earned in a course articulated to PSYC 1, the student must earn a minimum grade of B– (2.7) in a course articulated to PSYC 10 or PSYC 20. Refer to the “Recommended Preparation” section below for courses approved to fulfill these requirements.



AP Exam Score Substitutions

Qualifying AP exam scores may be used to satisfy certain required transfer screening courses. UCSC awards course credit based on the year the exam was taken. Exam credit policies are available on the Degree Progress Unit website.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required major preparation courses, prospective transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses prior to transfer may need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree.


Students who cannot enroll in the articulated equivalent of PSYC 20 at a California Community College are strongly encouraged to take PSYC 20 at UC Santa Cruz the summer before their first fall quarter. For more information, please visit the UC Santa Cruz Summer Edge webpage.


Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Visit the Psychology website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Psychology B.A. Program Statement
